mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization reflects supply-side tightening that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when capacity utilization exceeds 80%, or the signal reverses direction in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals weakening aggregate demand that prompts immediate portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling PAYEMS yields a positive 60-day forward return in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening labor demand that supports equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines fail to predict positive 20-day returns in post-recession periods, or the signal becomes negative when unemployment falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% monthly indicates robust supply-side momentum that supports near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 2%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
